title: 🇬🇧BLACK BRITS REACTS TO - Alabama State University Marching Band ft. The Stingettes - Marching In!
body:
Its nice to see Brits looking closer at ALL parts of the Black American Culture. These young ppl are at University studing to get thier degrees. They will be Doctors, Business people, Leaders and Trailblazers, all while showing thier other talents doing extra circulular stuff. Its the great HBCU expereince!

They are headed to the game on campus to support the school's football team. Looking good!

title: 🇬🇧BLACK BRITS REACTS TO - Alabama State University Marching Band ft. The Stingettes - Marching In!
body: Year after year, the HBSCs graduate a new field of educated black professionals.  These schools were vital during segregation in the USA, when all communities in the south needed black physicians, dentists, teachers, nurses, undertakers, etc.  And vital today for the preservation of black culture and history that it largely hidden from the world.
author: @DWB8072

title: 🇬🇧BLACK BRITS REACTS TO - Alabama State University Marching Band ft. The Stingettes - Marching In!
body: I would recommend watching more than just the march in with these HBCUs because the performances only go up from there. We go off and our culture is second to none! Have fun going down the rabbit hole of our rich culture! There are so many more HBCUs to see and culture to embrace! Much love!
author: @malayka85

title: Birmingham Alabama Pros and Cons | Moving to Birmingham AL | LIVING IN BIRMINGHAM ALABAMA
body:
My hometown! I love Birmingham and it will always have my heart.

It’s a ton of great restaurants, the people truly do get along, and the suburbs are really nice.
author: @TempeSoldier123

title: Birmingham Alabama Pros and Cons | Moving to Birmingham AL | LIVING IN BIRMINGHAM ALABAMA
body: Californian who just visited and wow was I blown away. So much nicer then I expected. Bottega was Absolutely all it's cracked up to be. Would choose moving there vs anywhere inland in CA.
author: @surfingcavachon

title: Birmingham Alabama Pros and Cons | Moving to Birmingham AL | LIVING IN BIRMINGHAM ALABAMA
body: A huge con for me is that mountain passes funnel traffic into narrow areas resulting in colossal traffic jams twice a day, and accidents can back up the limited access for double digit miles with hour or longer delays.
author: @justinsellers9402

title: Birmingham Alabama Pros and Cons | Moving to Birmingham AL | LIVING IN BIRMINGHAM ALABAMA
body: I was born in Birmingham in the early 1960s . It was a great place to grow up until the 1990s. I left in 2006 after muy residence got broken into the second time and I luckily dodged a bullet not figuratively but literally and my 72 year old uncle was murdered in a home invasion in one those great suberbs you are referring to. I now live in state about 1200 miles away and never intend on setting foot in the b'ham again. 😢
author: @lanceh.5671

title: Birmingham Alabama Pros and Cons | Moving to Birmingham AL | LIVING IN BIRMINGHAM ALABAMA
body:
I currently live in Las Vegas, but I have family moving to Laurel, MS, and I’m about to begin training in Oklahoma City to become an Air Traffic Controller. I’m aware that following said training, I’m gonna be dispatched to a facility that I’ll likely get the choice to choose from a list of available facilities around the country, and on a recent list that came out as plausible destinations, Birmingham was one of them, and it was high on the list! I thought, “Well, that’s highly interesting”, as that would put me close to where I have family relocating to (a few hours northeast), the piece on the airport here was really noteworthy, as for the FAA it’s a level 8 facility (perfect for trainees), but if it’s renovated, it might be a great spot to work in, and after weighing the pros & cons of this place, most of the cons I’m already used to here in Vegas (heat & crime), the storms don’t necessarily scare me, if anything they would excite me, and all the pros certainly make it seem like this city has more pros than cons that fit my lifestyle, and thus makes this an attractive destination for me!

Of course I’ve gotta get through the training before I ever can actually consider life in Birmingham, but after watching this, I think I’m really gonna look into it!

Thanks for posting this!
author: @Steve_Hunts96

title: Birmingham Alabama Pros and Cons | Moving to Birmingham AL | LIVING IN BIRMINGHAM ALABAMA

title: Alabama State University Freshman Move In Day 2023 VLOG, MLK Dorm Tour | ASU Mom's POV
body: Hey Tracie! I really loved this vlog! Congratulations to Isaiah on his graduation and entrance/move in to ASU! What a blessing and huge accomplishment! My husband and I watched as we ate breakfast this morning and we reminisced on when took our daughters to college. They are 4 years apart. I thought I was going to lose it… both times we dropped each of them off. They are now both college graduates and wonderful productive citizens with families and we couldn’t be any more proud of them than we are! But I feel you Sis! Those were some very sad but happy moments in time that we will never forget!

title: Check Out These Videos - alabama state cafe fight REVIEW AND MY THOUGHTS
body: Well, the whole cafe wasn't fighting, or running. The camera only shows the students that were involved. I didn't bother to look or run and join the melee. It's a shame because there are great students that attend Alabama State University, and this won't do those kids any justice. I am an honor student at ASU, and it pains me because people jump to conclusions over the actions of 40 clowns.
author: @DaVoYAGA7

title: Check Out These Videos - alabama state cafe fight REVIEW AND MY THOUGHTS
body: @yaphank  No, it didn't run out of watermelon, or fried chicken, or ribs, or any other racially suggested food. I am not sure what started it, but it's a shame. I am a student here, and this is not something I am proud of. The entire student body should not be judged by the actions of 40 clowns..
author: @DaVoYAGA7

title: Check Out These Videos - alabama state cafe fight REVIEW AND MY THOUGHTS
body: Last time I checked; Human are mammals.  And mammals fight.  Yes, we as humans have a more mature brain. But we're going to fight. The problem is when it's a minority group; then it's an issue & a problem.  For all the "pale skin" American's (who darken their skin to look like us), type in Baseball fights; Hockey fights; Crowd stadium fights.  And tell me who you see fighting then. Hell, there's a new video of pale face fighting at Denny's. But, there's no problem with that group fighting. Right
author: @alrober1906

title: It's Time to Be Honest About The 2023 Alabama State Stingettes | BOTB Review

title: It's Time to Be Honest About The 2023 Alabama State Stingettes | BOTB Review
body:
I been saying these same things, AS a longtime Stingette fan. As much as we hated on Bridgette, she is the glue that kept the Stingette dance programming(tradition, technique, style, cleanliness, overall development) tight. Amir was amazing at marketing and glam. They would've been a dynamic duo!
Over the years, I've learned a lot about the nuances of the Stingette program and this year completely threw me for a loop. I am very happy for Zy and I admire their hustle, drive, and commitment AND I can also say I wasn't into the direction of this years ensemble.
author: @toryjei9435

title: It's Time to Be Honest About The 2023 Alabama State Stingettes | BOTB Review
body: Look under all the vids and I been saying the same things and I get attacked. People wanted Zy to be good cuz she is pretty but ulta don’t win dance battles. She lacks leadership and creativity and it showed. She wasted music, did the same counts, mind you they dragged Lea last year for this, the team is sloppy and choppy. None of the finesse stings have. The walk and buck majority of field show, the two row formation was elementary…. I can go on and on about how disappointing her season was. I’m never afraid to express my opinion. She was an epic fail as captain

title: Cybertruck Towing RV: Alabama Gulf Coast Stops & Honest Reviews!
body: I’m really enjoying your campground reviews.  We stayed at Gulf state park last spring and loved it.  Hoping to get another reservation there soon.
author: @amysnyder210

title: Cybertruck Towing RV: Alabama Gulf Coast Stops & Honest Reviews!
body: Hey man, I was just looking in the 2517s and I imagine it’s the same on the 2519s, I found the factory inverter in the front driver side corner, behind a little panel, plenty of room in there for an all in one inverter. Maybe even another mppt. With the space under the murphy couch you can fit 15kW or more of lithium batteries, even if you don’t upgrade the solar panels or remove the 12v battery, you can charge up the lithium from the pedestal. May give you a way to charge the truck even if minimally if you run out of range on the road.
author: @NoTimeForThatNow

title: We Found Alabama's BEST Brisket Hash at Puckett's in Cullman! (Honest Review)
body: Glad you enjoyed Puckett's.  I have a couple near me in Columbia and Murfreesboro TN.   We have always enjoyed our visits, and the live music too.  My sister once ordered the Chicken fried Chicken and it was bigger than our heads!  It was picture worthy!
author: @Jeffery-v6i

title: We Found Alabama's BEST Brisket Hash at Puckett's in Cullman! (Honest Review)
body: There is a Puckett's in Pigeon Forge but we have not tried it. I never really thought about them having breakfast.  I'm an omelet lover so Leah's omelet looked delicious to me. The skillet looked very hearty. I enjoy spicy sauces but sometimes they can have an off taste. I've found this to be especially true when places have a lot of different heat level sauces. So I know exactly what you are talking about. Definitely go back for a dinner video. Thanks for another great video!
author: @vafunseekers

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: This black girl loves Alabama, and I don't see racism, I only see Southern pride. I believe some use the music as a symbol of hate but it's always been the rich pitting the white poor against the poor blacks, and brown. I feel the same pride that any white man feels when I listen to Alabama. It has nothing to do with race to me just pride in time and a way of life and most importantly the struggle.
author: @EvenderDowns

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: Family , work, honesty,  simple living, values, .. this is why so many people around the world like American country music. God  bless America and everything it stands for.
author: @gustavogentilin9894

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: 40+ years later my husband and I are still rollin' down a backwoods, Tennessee byway, one arm on the wheel. Life is good. Thank You Jesus.
author: @JackieH-h8c

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: 57 year old here actually crying to this song . Only because I know we can never go back to this time and music
author: @mike3813

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: 0:55 We dated for 2 years then got engaged. 6 months later we married. Our first date was on April 5th 1981. Our only child was born on April 5th 1985. We have been married for 43 years this October. We've been through some very rough times but the Lord Jesus is good and kept us strong. Praise the name of the Lord 🙌♥️🕊️
author: @dubyredburndarling6334

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: This is how the country looked when I was growing up. The cars and trucks, how they're dressed, that back road with the sun setting behind the trees, the old homes especially the one with all the chairs on the front porch and a clothes line out back, the tractor, the old store, even the saw mill. I watch this video often just to go back to a time when the world was a little kinder and sane.
author: @angelamcclure7905

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: As much as I love this song, can we take a moment to appreciate that cool old Chevy pickup? 4-doors weren't too common back then!

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: Growing up in Michigan, we loved Alabama's music. Their blue collar roots and rural America centered lyrics spoke to an entire generation all across the country.  I'm thankful for YouTube to be able to re-vist these moments in time. I can still recite so many of their songs lyrics even 40 years later. Sad to read of Jeff Cooks passing. I hope he and the rest of the band recognized the profound impact they had on so many.
author: @JamesReif

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: It absolutely gets no better than this....ever.  I'm 53 and wish more than ever I could go back to this time again.

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body:
Tennessee just beat Alabama 52-49. What a game. I’m a Georgia fan and I was emotionally attached to this game and seeing Bama lose is like Christmas.

Edit: Still relishing in Bama’s loss 😂
Edit (again): Y’all actually thought y’all were gonna beat us (UGA)? Think again 😂. GG’s though!

body: For everyone saying she is a kid of a rich family. She runs all of her social media platforms alone which make you gain a lot of money. Her BMW is a hand me down from her mom from 2010. Most of her skincare is PR. She does really nice videos about all of the unboxing and that is where most of her skincare and make up is from. In her Amazon storefront you can see some of the things that she used to decorate her room and they do not cost an insane amount of money. I think we should be happy for her and also appreciate the things that we have in life, without looking so much into others people life and choosing either to say if the person grew up rich or poor.

author: @squidwardwithblondehair7219

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: I’m Asian…and I wasn’t offended by the lady in the chair at all. The lady in the chair didn’t seem to have any ill-will. She unfortunately just was never taught the best way to use the correct words and terminology with people of Asian descent/other ethnicities, or was never taught how to properly interact with people of other ethnicities in general. Her choice of words were really poor…but she had her heart in a good place and it definitely wasn’t “heckling”.
author: @fd3

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: The younger generation is usually more welcoming, but one thing to point out is that people behave differently to their true self when they know they’re being filmed
author: @lychee0733

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: Going to a Walmart in the most racist town in America is a terrible idea, but it helps to expose discrimination. Huge praise to you guys for being so brave!
author: @MahiAviation

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: You guys are so brave...I was born and raised in Chicago, and although most areas are diverse, I always got weird stares and occasional racist comments from strangers and classmates. I transferred to WIU in Macomb around covid time and omfg, I should've known but people would take one look at me and run away lol I'm glad you guys were welcomed and had a good time in Arkansas!
author: @enkarion01

title: Prison State (full documentary) | FRONTLINE
body: That Vietnam vet was sent to fight a war for a country that never appreciated his mental health. 40+ years later we're still in the same position.
author: @dreamrae

title: Prison State (full documentary) | FRONTLINE
body: When they closed down the Psychiatric Hospitals, they created this problem.  The first guy has Bipolar Disorder w/ Schizophrenia.  Those diagnoses require medication management for life.  He has problems managing his life, doesn't have the coping skills.  There's no place for him in our society, so he will cycle in and out of prison until his Mental Health improves or he commits a crime serious enough that he never leaves prison.  When I worked in the Texas State Prison system, I saw many who had pathology.  Some were mentally aware enough to change their behavior and some did not have the ability to change.  Unfortunately, there is no place for those who are falling through the cracks.  America doesn't care "enough" about our mentally ill to help (funding).  It's more profitable to incarcerate than to rehabilitate.
author: @DIVISIONINCISION

title: Prison State (full documentary) | FRONTLINE

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: One word for this video, Tranquility.  The scenes and sounds give a calming effect to the viewers.  Thank you for presenting a half hour of visible peacefulness.
author: @piperleelee

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Thank you so much for the elegant simplicity in your videos. My father in law comes from a long line of farmers, and now, at 84, he's dying of cancer. He's mostly deaf, so keeping him entertained is a struggle. I had the idea to put farming videos on his tablet that he could just watch and not need to worry about dialogue. His imagination can fill in all the animal sounds. He smiled more broadly at this video than at anything else in the recent past. It's like you've given him his young life back, one video at a time. We thank you. ❤
author: @ACarole

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Okay this was just BEAUTIFUL. Omg... the heavy fog, the lush green grass, the cows looking nothing short of mjestic, Morgan riding his bike with the LWFD (Large white farm dog)running behind him.. this was just perfect Morgan. Please do more videos like this... this was beautiful
author: @Alicia-fy3je

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: This is what all farms should look like. The madness of factory farming and overproduction is disgusting. This is just beautiful.
author: @bumble8145

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: 24:07 Hey that's me!! It was so amazing to meet you and everyone on the farm Morgan! Peaches the goose is doing wonderful.
author: @mtorrisi42

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: I still can not believe that I've found this video, it is truly an enormous luck. It gives you so much so much to think about and calm your mind down at the same time: what a miracle. Slow pace of life of farm is just charming, but it will be inevitable for normal big city-people to let their souls relax a little bit, that's what frustrating. It is a heaven on earth, I nearly cried, frankly speaking I shed dozens of tears... I was touched by the fact that you give them all names, that you treat them so kindly. A very cautious and gentle man - rarity in modern world. Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
author: @qwo1x222

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: I love seeing how excited the animals get to see you, you clearly have a bond with them all. Your everyday chores and jobs are daily things for you Morgan. For us it's like going on an adventure, and likely one we will never experience in real life.
author: @lms1068

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Morgan, the new barn structure is beautiful. I confess I do love the "weird" chickens moment of Zen. Lady Abbyington sure enjoys running alongside you, as you ride your e-bike up to the cattle herd. It seems Amanda Hugginkiss likes her solitude. Its great to see everyone and Allison's kitchen garden, too. Take care!

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
So glad to see that the tadpoles became adult frogs. Congratulations! Yah! All the cows will get a chance to mingle next week. It was so good to see the wife and garden. Please say hello to her for me. Thanks!
Prayers for health and safety.🙏❤🙏
author: @katherinemclean7103

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: I grew up with my grandmother in 🇰🇪, Kenya, Mukurweini Nyeri. These are the sounds I would wake upto for most of my life. Now at 30 years and living in cold and depressed Sweden 🇸🇪 with my family,  I desperately want to have my children experience what peace and tranquility I had while growing up.  Thank you for this video❤
author: @Summerrain55

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
I grew up in this situation.
My father is still a farmer.
So beautiful life.
Now, after I graduate from college and live in a crowded city and huge traffic environment, I feel like we all need such a calm, natural, and beautiful life ❤
Thank you for sharing your story with us

body: Beautiful video. Sounds, sights and rhythms of the farm. The foggy day makes it even more peaceful and soothing to watch. Like how you are letting Toby model good behavior with the baby birds. Abby is learning. Thanks for this video!

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
The otherworldlyness of the farm on a foggy summer morning is breathtaking.
It looked like your farm bike was going to suddenly run into Rod Serling as he talked about the rest of your day.
author: @VonPatzy

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
It was a real treat to enjoy the farm from your perspective, Morgan. No jets flying overhead, no cars or trucks racing down the road. Just the bucolic sounds of nature; grass rustling, baby geese chirping, pigs snorting, macho man mooing, you talking to your animals, them talking back.
The peace de resistance: frogs swimming and croaking in the night, in the pond that you created for them, from the eggs you protected from the birds. Your reward: a nightly symphony of the life you facilitated because nothing says farm and country like the wildlife that emerges from the pond, on a warm summer night in the month of June.
Peace, from Green-Eyed Lady.
author: @scooterpush

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: I had to laugh when the goslings wouldn't go into the duck house. That was cool watching the frogs swimming in the pond. Thank you.

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Very nice and soothing video. I loved everything but especially the moment with Abby standing among the cows in the green pasture with the pretty and dainty looking yellow flowers. Reminds me of portrait. Thank you Morgan for sharing yourself with us your hopes and dreams coming true in a beautiful farm and a productive yet meaningful life. It brings back memories of youth when I was able to be in the countryside away from busy Boston in the summertime. Those are wonderful memories you brought back to me. Thank you for such nostalgic gift. Keep on doing what you do the best, being creative and humane in your farm life.

Such an inspiring space to study. Sorority row was impressive. I guess I didn’t expect much thinking they would be  run of the mill Greek houses(big, but a little tired, needs help). title: UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA *CAMPUS TOUR* | Classrooms, Sorority Row, Stadium, Starbucks, Library, Dorms
body:
Many things changed, since I was a student at UoA (1977-1984), the ΔΔΔ house was across the street from where it is now and it was round.
Barnwell field, that we used to play soccer is now a parking lot.
But the ΔΓ anchor, even with it's new brass color (it was dark colored in my days) reminded me of a time that our ΘΤ (back then it was a men's fraternity and ΔΓs were our Little Sisters, I think this changed now because in the new form ΘΤ became coed because is was a professional engineering fraternity and I believe the old scheme was dissolved for a while) pledges hand cuffed me on the anchor and as our members were chasing them around, in order to get the handcuff's key to free me, they lost the key in the grass lol.
I got so mad with the whole thing, that I put the double side of the cuff my hand was tied with, over a blade near the top of the ΔΓ anchor and using my arm as a lever, I broke the pin of the hand cuffs and set my self free, with only minor cuts on the sides of my wrist.
So the ΔΓ anchor has it's history with me too lol.
author: @FLORATOSOTHON

title: Alabama State University Montgomery Alabama HBCU College Campus Tour
body: The first few seconds captivated me. I had to go back and see the intro again! Superb editing ! The intro , the music and sounds matching the timing of each frame is impressive . I love the music choices from the band you chose . This video also gave a lot of information about the school, I learned of some greats that went to ASU. I also appreciated how you talked about the work you have to do and how HBCU’s have to be more prominent with our youth, because the numbers aren’t lying. You made great points about DEI and affirmative action and the fact that it’s going away with this new presidential administration, and how this will affect the admissions process for PWI’s and open up more HBCU student opportunities. Wonderful work 👏🏽🫶🏽 All around 10 stars for this video
author: @tamarahnate

title: Alabama State University Montgomery Alabama HBCU College Campus Tour
body: My daughter has been admitted for Fall 2025 . I really looked forward to it but even with 1410, we couldn't get enough funding to realize the Hornet dream😢
author: @ugochinyereegbe5998
